coredata.dc:title: A dual-mode pH sensor film based on the pyrene-based Zr-MOF self-destruction with fluorescence turn-on effect
coredata.dc:creator: Li, Hangzhou
Chen, Qiumeng
Wang, Yuanyuan
Zhang, Zhixiao
Chen, Huan
Wang, Zhaoli
Gong, Zhengjun
coredata.dc:description: Rapid detection of on-the-spot water sample pH has become an urgent problem to resolve, and developing a method with the characters of fast, convenient and low-cost is significant. In this study, an amino functionalized pyrene-based Zr-MOF (NU-1000-NH2) was synthesized by solvent-assisted ligand incorporation (SALI) method, in which the Zr6 metal cluster, fluorescent pyrene-based H4TBAPy and p-aminobenzoic acid were acted as node, linker and modulator, respectively. The as-synthesized NU-1000-NH2 possessed the pH-dependent fluorescence enhancement property at 450 nm emission under alkaline condition, thus a convenient fluorescence “turn-on” pH sensor was fabricated. Upon introduction of OH–, the carboxylate groups in NU-1000-NH2 could react with it, and the deprotonation and gradual collapse of the NU-1000-NH2 crystal were occurred, resulting in the release of the fluorescent H4TBAPy ligand and fluorescence enhancement. Moreover, to solve the difficulties of on-the-spot pH detection, the solid-phase pH film sensor was successfully constructed by embedding the NU-1000-NH2 particles into the polyvinyl alcohol (PVA), combining with the RGB model and smartphone detector. The proposed fluorescent MOF-based pH sensor with dual-mode detection is expected to achieve on-the-spot pH monitoring in the field of environmental monitoring.
coredata.dcterms:subject: H4TBAPy
Metal organic frameworks
“turn-on” fluorescence
pH film sensor

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Characterization of NU-1000-NH2 and film sensor
Fluorescence properties of ligand and NU-1000-NH2

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: This work is financially supported by the Science and Technology Department Foundation of Sichuan Province (No. 2022YFS0502, 2022NSFC0989 & 2022024) and the National Natural Science Foundation of China (No. 22176154). Thanks to the Analytical and Testing Center of Southwest Jiaotong University for the support of experimental conditions.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (a) Powder X-ray diffraction (PXRD) analysis and (b) SEM of as-synthesized NU-1000-NH2; (c) photograph and (d, e) SEM of NU-1000-NH2/PVA pH-dependent film sensors; (f) FTIR spectrums of ligand H4TBAPy, NU-1000, NU-1000-NH2 and NU-1000-NH2/PVA film.

Fluorescence emission spectrums of same liquid concentration of ligand H4TBAPy and NU-1000-NH2, excited at 370 nm; the pH values of the two solutions of ligand H4TBAPy and NU-1000-NH2 were 4.35 and 4.86, respectively; insert: solid-state powder of H4TBAPy and NU-1000-NH2 under daylight and 365 nm UV light.

Fluorescence response spectrums and the emission intensities in the range of pH = 1–13 of (a) NU-1000-NH2, and (b) H4TBAPy. And concentrations of NU-1000-NH2 and H4TBAPy were 0.2 mg·mL−1 and 0.04 mg·mL−1, respectively.

(a) Fluorescence spectrums of NU-1000-NH2 in blank (pH = 4.51), pH = 7.45, 10.06 and 13.27 (under the Ex/Em bandwidth of 1.5 nm of the fluorescence spectrometer), insert: samples under 365 nm UV light. (b) NU-1000-NH2 suspension samples in alkaline condition under daylight and 365 nm UV light.

(a) FL intensity of various trace anions selectivity of NU-1000-NH2, and the concentrations of various anions were 100 μM; (b) fluorescence response spectrums of NU-1000-NH2 in dense alkaline pH, insert: 365 nm UV light samples of lower concentration of NU-1000-NH2 suspension in the range of pH = 7–13; (c) relationship between the relative intensity (regard the strongest intensity in pH = 13 as 1.0) and pH value; (d) the linear fitting curve.
Sketch map of the deprotonation process upon the increasing OH– concentration in alkaline pH condition, the deprotonated MOF “turned on” the light blue fluorescence.

(a) fluorescence response of pH film sensors reacting with pH from 7 to 13, under 365 nm UV light; (b) averaged RGB component values of each sensor in the presence of different pH and (c) the linear fitting plot of adjusted intensity and pH value.
Comparison of pH detection performances based on reported fluorescent sensors.
Results and RSD values of sensing pH in real water samples using proposed methods.

head.ce:title: A dual-mode pH sensor film based on the pyrene-based Zr-MOF self-destruction with fluorescence turn-on effect

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Faculty of Geosciences and Environmental Engineering, Southwest Jiaotong University, Chengdu 611756, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Faculty of Geosciences and Environmental Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Faculty of Geosciences and Environmental Engineering, Southwest Jiaotong University, Chengdu 611756, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Biogeochemistry & Environmental Quality Research Group, Clemson University, SC 29442, United States

ce:affiliation.ce:label: c
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Chengdu Academy of Environmental Sciences, Chengdu 610072, China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Chengdu Academy of Environmental Sciences, Chengdu 610072, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: d
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: State-province Joint Engineering Laboratory of Spatial Information Technology of High-Speed Rail Safety, Chengdu 611756, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: State-province Joint Engineering Laboratory of Spatial Information Technology of High-Speed Rail Safety

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: State-province Joint Engineering Laboratory of Spatial Information Technology of High-Speed Rail Safety, Chengdu 611756, China

ce:correspondence.ce:text: Corresponding author at: Faculty of Geosciences and Environmental Engineering, Southwest Jiaotong University, Chengdu 611756, China (Z. Gong).

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Faculty of Geosciences and Environmental Engineering

ce:footnote.ce:note-para: The first two authors contributed equally to this work.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Rapid detection of on-the-spot water sample pH has become an urgent problem to resolve, and developing a method with the characters of fast, convenient and low-cost is significant. In this study, an amino functionalized pyrene-based Zr-MOF (NU-1000-NH2) was synthesized by solvent-assisted ligand incorporation (SALI) method, in which the Zr6 metal cluster, fluorescent pyrene-based H4TBAPy and p-aminobenzoic acid were acted as node, linker and modulator, respectively. The as-synthesized NU-1000-NH2 possessed the pH-dependent fluorescence enhancement property at 450 nm emission under alkaline condition, thus a convenient fluorescence “turn-on” pH sensor was fabricated. Upon introduction of OH–, the carboxylate groups in NU-1000-NH2 could react with it, and the deprotonation and gradual collapse of the NU-1000-NH2 crystal were occurred, resulting in the release of the fluorescent H4TBAPy ligand and fluorescence enhancement. Moreover, to solve the difficulties of on-the-spot pH detection, the solid-phase pH film sensor was successfully constructed by embedding the NU-1000-NH2 particles into the polyvinyl alcohol (PVA), combining with the RGB model and smartphone detector. The proposed fluorescent MOF-based pH sensor with dual-mode detection is expected to achieve on-the-spot pH monitoring in the field of environmental monitoring.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: The data that has been used is confidential.

ce:section.ce:para: The pH value is regarded as a fundamental and significant parameter in the fields of environmental analysis and chemical reactions, in particularly the rapid quality detection of water sample [1–3]. For the water environment, the changes and fluctuations in pH value may cause harmful and irreversible damage for the livings. So, it is very necessary to explore and develop the method of real-time monitoring of pH. Metal organic frameworks (MOFs), a sort of porous and crystalline materials with the assembly of metal cluster and organic ligands [4], have appeared lots of prospective applications in luminescence, catalysis degradation, bioanalysis, molecular delivery and separation [4–6]. Measurements of electrochemical method to detect pH value are the most common and usually used, such as potentiometer, voltammeter and current [7], while the shortcomings of high cost and difficult assembly also promoted us to develop a more suitable method. Luminescent MOFs were appropriately used in the quantitative optical detection of metal ions, anions, pH, organic molecules and etc. Fluorescence monitoring takes the advantages of rapid detection, convenience, low cost and visual monitoring by naked eyes [8–10]. And optical monitoring pH value based on fluorescent-MOFs has become a promising detection method due to the pH-sensitive components of its ligands, metal clusters and functionalized groups [11].

Gu et al. [12] synthesized HNU-39, a uranium-based MOF accompanied by in situ ligand formation of H4DTATC [5,5′-(9, 10-dihydroxy-4a, 9,9a,10-tetrahydroanthracene-9, 10-diyl)- diisophthalic acid)], in which the carboxylic acid groups deprotonated reacting with OH– to realize the fluorescence turn-on pH sensor. While, at the same time, Aguilera-Sigalat [13] and Kandiah et al. [14] both reported that the MOF will be in certain structural collapse or complete decomposition damage after being soaked in the condition of alkaline solution. Although the research of pH sensors has made some progress, the pH sensors based on the optical property of MOFs is worthy of further study in on-the-spot monitoring, visual detection and etc.

As one of the most widely used aromatic hydrocarbons and fluorophores, especially in construction of MOFs, pyrene has become an excellent ligand candidate for its good photoluminescence emission properties with long lived excited-state lifetime [15–17]. 1,3,6,8-Tetrakis (4-carboxylphenyl) pyrene (H4TBAPy), has attracted lots of interests and been the most studied ligand for synthesis of pyrene-MOFs due to its relative rigid pyrene core. And its carboxylate groups with certain rotational freedom degree located on the 1-, 3-, 6- and 8- positions are free to link to the metal cluster of MOF [18]. Promising structural and optical characteristics of H4TBAPy made it suitable to response to the guest molecules for fluorescent-MOF design [19], which showed great potential in the field of fluorescence application.

In this study, we used the Zr6 metal cluster and ligand of H4TBAPy to construct a newly pyrene-based Zr-MOF with the introduction of the amino alkaline group, namely, NU-1000-NH2. The synthesized fluorescent-MOF showed the performances of fluorescence turn-on in alkaline, and turn-off in acidic conditions, indicating that the interaction between H+/OH– in aqueous media and the alkaline/acidic groups of MOF could realize the fluorescent pH sensing. The fluorescence turn-on in alkaline following with the gradual collapse of MOF crystal and its release of ligand were observed and detailed discussed. Moreover, to develop a feasible method of pH film sensor, especially under the condition of on-the-spot pH monitoring and rapid detection, we herein managed to implant the MOF onto the solid substrate of polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) for that it is convenient to form the solid shape with smooth surface. And to reach the visual detection, we explored the RGB calculation method by extracting the RGB value of the captured photos under 365 nm UV light, which established an equation with the pH value, so that it can be used as semi-quantitative analysis for rapid pH value measurement. It is noted that the rational construction of MOF showed its potential to become the alkaline pH sensor and can be detected by naked eyes based on fluorescence turn-on effect.

All the chemical materials and reagents are the highest purity without further purification, and were purchased from commercial suppliers, 1,3,6,8-Tetrakis (4-carboxylphenyl) pyrene (H4TBAPy), was directly obtained from Adamas Reagent Co., ltd. And the liquid organics of N, N-dimethylformamide (DMF), acetone and ethanol absolute are of analytical grade, ≥98 %, and purchased from Kelong Chemical Co., ltd. (Chengdu, China).

All the instruments employed for the fabrication of MOFs and characterization are Ultrasonic cleaner (GT-SonicD9, China), Vacuum drying oven (DZF-6050, China), Centrifuge (TD-420, China), JSM-7800F scanning electron microscope (SEM) (Hitachi, Japan), Fourier-transform infrared spectroscopy (FT-IR) (PerkinElmer, USA), X’ Pert PRO X-ray diffraction (XRD) (Thermo Fisher Scientific, USA), USB-2000 UV–vis spectrometer (Ocean Optics, USA), PHS-3 W pH meter (Inesa, China) and FLS1000 steady-state/transient fluorescence spectrometer (Edinburgh, UK). All the visual detection was performed under ultraviolet light at 365 nm, and the fluorescence color photos under 365 nm UV light were recorded by a smartphone (Huawei nova3i, CHN).

The synthesis of NU-1000-NH2 is based on the parent NU-1000 [20] by method of solvent-assisted ligand incorporation (SALI). Briefly, 32 mg (0.014 mmol) of synthesized NU-1000 was added into a 100 mL glass beaker, and 65 mg (0.474 mmol) of p-aminobenzoic acid (PABA) was weighted out to add into the same beaker, afterwards 45 mL of DMF was added to fully disperse powder NU-1000 and mix PABA to an even suspension, and sonicated for more than 20 min to totally dissolve all the reagents, then the reaction was placed in 80 °C oven for 24 h. The light yellow precipitate was obtained by centrifuge after reaction cooling down to ambient temperature, the supernatant of reaction was decanted from the centrifugal tube. The obtained resultant was then washed with fresh DMF more than 3 times, 2 h for each time, which was then centrifuged to have the precipitate. The NU-1000-NH2 is obtained after 12 h of 100 °C heating dried in air.

Preparation of pH film sensor: weigh out 1.1 g polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) to fully dissolve in 20 mL H2O with stirring and sonicate for 15 min, then add 16 mg of as-synthesized NU-1000-NH2 powder into the PVA solution. To fully dissolve the mixed solution, stir and sonicate for another 20 min until the light-yellow solution formed. Then, the mixture was placed at 90 ℃ with magnetically stirring for about 1 h to generate the homogenous gelatinous liquid, and transferred the resultant mixture on the inner surface of 100 mm petri dish evenly, and dried the dish at 90 ℃ for another 1 h until the smooth light-yellow solid film formed. The obtained NU-1000-NH2/PVA film was transferred out of petri dish and cut into 10 × 10 mm solid smooth unit films with thickness about 2 mm.

All the fluorescence sensing experiments were conducted under ambient temperature, and the data of fluorescence spectrums were collected on FLS1000 steady-state/transient fluorescence spectrometer (Edinburgh, UK). For each sample of pH quantitative detection, 50 μL of fluorescence probe of 0.02 mg·mL−1 and 950 μL of pH buffer (HCl/H2PO4 -/HPO4 2-/NaOH) were added into a 1.5 mL centrifugal tube to fully dissolve and mix to obtain a sample of 1 mL capacity. For the visualization experiments, all the samples of at least 1 mL capacity in 1.5 mL centrifugal tube were placed under the 365 nm UV light platform, and the color photos were captured by smartphone camera under the dark environment, the same camera parameters were set for each photo capture of visualization experiments.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Characterization of NU-1000-NH2 and film sensor
ce:section.ce:para: NU-1000-NH2 was synthesized based on its parent MOF, i.e. NU-1000 by incorporating the alkaline –NH2 functional group of p-aminobenzoic acid using SALI method. While, the structure still remains the csq topology as well as NU-1000 with mesoporous hexagonal channels [21], which consists of H4TBAPy linker and 8-connected Zr6 secondary-building units (SBUs) [21–24]. The powder X-ray diffraction (PXRD) analysis of as-synthesized NU-1000-NH2 showed the good pattern with two strong peaks (2θ = 5.2° and 7.5°, Fig. 1 a), indicating that the obtained powder kept pure phase. SEM was employed to further investigate the morphology, in which the rod-shaped of NU-1000-NH2 was clearly presented (Fig. 1b). The 10 × 10 mm NU-1000-NH2/PVA film with thickness about 2 mm were obtained after the cutting, and the solid smooth unit films were shown in Fig. 1c. The rod-shaped NU-1000-NH2 particles were stabilized and well-embedded in the solid PVA substrate film under the observation of SEM (Fig. 1d and 1e).

The FTIR spectrums of ligand H4TBAPy, NU-1000, NU-1000-NH2 and NU-1000-NH2/PVA film were recorded in Fig. 1f, in which the OH, CH and CO stretching vibrations at 3420 cm−1, 2930 cm−1 and 1409/1112 cm−1 [25–27] appeared in all the spectrums, a broad characteristic band 3200–3400 cm−1 of NU-1000-NH2 and NU-1000-NH2/PVA film was ascribed to the stretching vibrations of OH and NH [28], sharp bands at 1719 cm−1 for spectrum of H4TBAPy, 1660 cm−1 for spectrums of NU-1000-NH2 and NU-1000-NH2/PVA film, were the characteristic peaks of CO group [29,30]. And the band at 1580 cm−1 exhibited was the stretching vibrations of COO– [31]. The UV/Vis spectrums of H4TBAPy and NU-1000-NH2 shown in Fig. S1 appeared the same sharp peaks of absorbance band around 200 nm, and it was due to π→π* transition of the aromatic sp2 domains of ligand [32].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fluorescence properties of ligand and NU-1000-NH2
ce:section.ce:para: To assess the photoluminescence properties of pyrene ligand and synthesized pyrene-based Zr-MOF, NU-1000-NH2, fluorescence spectrometer was conducted under the same condition of excitation and emission bandwidth to study the fluorescence spectrums of two suspension solution at same concentration at 0.1 mg·mL−1. It is noted that the excitation spectrum of NU-1000-NH2 exhibited a broad peak between 300 nm and 420 nm with a shoulder peak at 370 nm, which can be regarded as the optimal excitation (Fig. S2). In comparison, Fig. 2 clearly demonstrated the emission spectrums difference, the ligand showed the strong intensity with 530 nm optimal emission, which is in concordance with the reported [33] and proved the excellent luminescence properties of pyrene. The emission intensity of synthesized NU-1000-NH2 was with optimal emission located on 520 nm. The solid-state powder and the fluorescence pictures under daylight and 365 nm ultraviolet light are depicted in Fig. 2 insert, which showed the distinct change of intensities and fluorescence color under UV due to the difference of optimal emission. So, it is interesting to explore the practical application of fluorescence turn-on pH detection based on the darker fluorescence intensity of NU-1000-NH2. Moreover, compared with the fluorescence quenching, the fluorescence enhancement of turn-on sensor is actually a better option and preferred for that it could reduce the possibility of false positives [34].

The protonation/deprotonation effect reaction of groups on MOFs under the condition of acidic/alkaline environment would greatly influence and change the fluorescence performance [35–38]. Encouraged by the acidic/alkaline groups of NU-1000-NH2, we investigated the fluorescence response of NU-1000-NH2 in different acidic/alkaline pH condition. In typical experiments, the buffer solution of pH = 1–13 were added to 50 μL suspension of a few milligrams solid powder to investigate whether the pyrene-based Zr-MOF can be employed as potential pH detection probe, and the emission spectrums of fluorescence performance were recorded under ambient temperature.

As shown in Fig. 3 a, the fluorescence intensity of NU-1000-NH2 in acidic pH appeared very low, but a gradual increasing intensity was observed as pH moved from 7 to 13, and the fluorescence “turn on” effect is reached. To make a comparison, the fluorescence performance of ligand H4TBAPy were also investigated in the range of pH = 1–13 (Fig. 3b), interestingly, same phenomenon of fluorescence intensity enhancement in alkaline environment of pH = 7–13 was obtained. As discussed above, this can be ascribed to the deprotonation of oxygen of carboxylate groups in the pyrene ligand and pyrene-based Zr-MOF, especially at a certain alkaline, which may destroy the conjugate system and the electron transfer [12,39,40]. FTIR was employed to analysis the stretching variation change of the mentioned two materials in acidic/alkaline pH, respectively. As shown in Fig. S3, the –COOH characteristic stretching variation around at 1645 cm−1 for NU-1000-NH2 and 1681 cm−1 for ligand H4TBAPy, has disappeared or diminished in alkaline condition, which indicates the existence of deprotonation effects.

Moreover, a distinguished fluorescence response in pH = 7 differed from NU-1000-NH2 was clearly presented on ligand H4TBAPy, that an abrupt increased transition of intensity phenomenon was observed, while for NU-1000-NH2, the increasing of intensity is gradual. No obvious increasing change of ligand H4TBAPy intensity in the range of pH = 8–11 showed in Fig. 3b. It is noted that, compared with the abrupt and nonlinear fluorescence turn-on response of the ligand H4TBAPy in alkaline pH, NU-1000-NH2 showed the great potential to become alkaline pH sensing probe. It is speculated that the construction of MOF although weakened the fluorescence intensity of ligand, the acidic group of MOF interacting with OH– is not as strong reactive as the ligand. So, MOF can realize the linear alkaline pH sensing due to its release of ligand when reacting in alkaline condition, and it can be concluded from the SEM and PXRD when the MOF particles soaked in alkaline pH [41], in which the gradual collapse of crystal was observed (Fig. S4 and S5). And the fluorescent response of the parent NU-1000 to alkaline pH was also investigated in Fig. S6, the fluorescence intensity kept increasing from pH 6.8 to 10.5, but its intensity began to fall from pH 10.5 to 13.3, which makes it difficult to determine the true pH. Herein, it is speculated that, companying with partial destruction, the NH group on the amino-functionalized MOF (NU-1000-NH2) will be deprotonated as well in alkaline pH [42], especially when the pH greater than 10.5, thus it caused the continuous increasing of fluorescence intensity.

Furthermore, in detailed study, we found that, even under a micro concentration of NU-1000-NH2 suspension, the fluorescence enhancement in alkaline environment could still perform excellent and a phenomenon of emission “blue shift” from 520 nm to around 450 nm was observed. As shown in Fig. 4 a, compared with the emission spectrum of the blank sample (pH = 4.51), more than 10 times of enhancement intensity and emission blue shift were recorded in pH = 7.45, 10.06 and 13.27, as the insert picture showed under 365 nm UV light. A blue shift of about 70 nm in emission peak along with increase of fluorescence intensity also dates back to the deprotonation of the carboxylate groups of ligand [43,44], and in the process of deprotonation, partial destruction of the pyrene-based Zr-MOF were monitored by PXRD and SEM in Fig. S4 and S5, revealing that the fluorescence enhancement in alkaline comes with the partial destruction of MOF crystal and ligand release [41]. And Fig. 4b showed the samples of NU-1000-NH2 probe in different alkaline pH under daylight and UV light, in which the turn-on bright light-blue fluorescence was demonstrated, and it gives out potential opportunity for the visualization of pH sensing by naked eyes in the following study.

To further study the pH-dependent detection fluorescence probe, and based on the observed fluorescence enhancement in alkaline environment, the luminescent selectivity of NU-1000-NH2 towards OH– among various trace anions was evaluated. As shown in Fig. 5 a, an apparent intensity enhancement of NU-1000-NH2 in trace concentration OH– clearly presented, and the remaining common anions of Br-, Cl-, I-, NO3 – and SO4 2- kept almost unchanged compared with the blank intensity. It is easy to understand that CO3 2– and HPO4 2- increased the intensity to some extent, because of the higher hydrolysis ionization constant of CO3 2– and HPO4 2-, OH– ionized from H2O in the hydrolysis process to react with NU-1000-NH2, which increased the fluorescence intensity. And the product ions of primary hydrolysis ionization, i.e. HCO3 – and H2PO4 - are weaker to hydrolyze to ionize OH–, so both of them cannot cause the obvious intensity increase as shown in Fig. 5a. It is noted that NU-1000-NH2 has good sensitivity and selectivity of OH–, which promoted us to explore its potential as pH-dependent detection fluorescence probe. To make the detection phenomenon more accurate and evident, we continued to lower the probe concentration, and still strong fluorescence enhancement was observed. As shown in Fig. 5b, more intensive and dense alkaline pH were employed to investigate the fluorescence performance of NU-1000-NH2, and the insert in Fig. 5b showed the samples of gradual change of blue light brightness under 365 nm UV light. As the result showed, the fluorescence intensity gradually increased by the increasing of pH from 7.0 to 13.0, and there is a good relationship between fluorescence intensity and pH in Fig. 5c. A simple calculated linear relative intensity-pH plot was established in the Fig. 5d, with a slope of 0.139, whose R2 value reached to 0.973 in the range of pH = 7.0–10.5, and the sensing process of NU-1000-NH2 to pH can be completed within 20 s.

Moreover, the UV–vis absorption spectra of NU-1000-NH2 and ligand H4TBAPy have been recorded in different alkaline condition of pH = 7–13 to compare with their blank solution. As shown in Fig. S7, two new adsorption peaks around 300 nm and 390 nm appeared compared with the absorption spectra of blank, but for NU-1000-NH2, in weak alkaline pH of 7.12 and 8.10, the new absorption peak firstly appeared around 260 nm and then shifted to around 300 nm with the rising of pH, it may explain the phenomenon of gradual fluorescence intensity increasing instead of an abrupt increased transition. The appearance of new adsorption peaks can be attributed to the n→π* transitions [32], the absorption peak at 300 nm increased with the pH rising of OH– concentration, which is speculated to the change of π system and the enhancement of n→π* transitions upon the deprotonation of ligand [35]. The deprotonated group on ligand will gradually improve the n orbit energy and cause the emission transition state change from π→π* to n→π* [45,46], thus the fluorescence intensity gradually and significantly increased, and the deprotonation process of the pyrene-based Zr-MOF is illustrated in Fig. 6 . NU-1000-NH2 realized the fluorescence turn-on in alkaline, and turn-off in acidic conditions, which is probably due to the deprotonation and protonation of the carboxyl and amino functional groups, respectively [47–49]. And the excellent fluorescence performance towards pH makes it become a pH-dependent probe for practical liquid pH detection.

Since the excellent sensitivity and selectivity of NU-1000-NH2 towards the trace OH– ion, which behaved the potential of on-the-spot visualization detection of pH, we managed to implant the powder onto a solid-substrate for convenient take-away. Serval methods including non-fluorescent filter paper-based, polyacrylonitrile (PAN), polyvinyl pyrrolidone (PVP), and polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) solution were compared to adopt the best performance of on-the-spot detection. As shown in Fig. S8, the PVP and PAN solid-substrates after heating were hard to form the shaped film compared with PVA-substrate. The visual alkaline pH detection under 365 nm UV of PVP and PAN substrates was observed the same fluorescence turn-on phenomenon, while it is not distinguishing to recognize only based the change of intensity. And non-fluorescent filter paper was soaked into the MOF suspension to distribute on the surface and heated to dried to prepare the paper-based sensor. As shown in Fig. S9, the change of fluorescence intensity was distinguishing, but the inhomogeneous distribution of luminescent MOF particles may misinform.

PVA was chosen for its good hydrophilicity with the hydroxyl groups on its side chains. And the prepared film sensor was employed to detect pH value of samples by adding the buffer pH solution (less than 100 μL) on the surface of the sensor. To our delight, as shown in Fig. 7 a, the bright-blue fluorescence emerged under 365 nm UV light, and the gradual increasing and enhancement of the brightness were obtained as pH moved from 7 to 13, which directly proved that the synthesis and application of the pH film sensor are successful and valid.

Moreover, for the convenience of on-the-spot pH detection, we established a “RGB method” by extracting the RGB value of the pictures of each sensor on different pH, the pictures were taken by the camera of smart phone with same photographing parameters sitting for each time to make sure the brightness comparison was controlled under the same background condition, and the RGB extracting was completed on Adobe Photoshop 9.0. Randomlyfive points in the picture were selected to extract their RGB values and then averaged all the values to calculate the IR , IG and IB component values, and also, the common background of blank sensor was subtracted. So, we established the equation of the adjusted intensity IAD as follow [10,50,51]:
I
AD
= 100 ×
I R
+
I G
+
I B
I
0 R
+
I
0 G
+
I
0 B
- 1

in which the IR , IG , IB and I0R , I0G , I0B represent the RGB component values of each pH detection sensor and the blank sensor, respectively. As shown in Fig. 7b, the averaged RGB intensities from each pH detection sensor were clearly presented, and the fitting linear plot relationship of IAD and pH values was established with R2 reached 0.991 in the range of pH = 7–11 (Fig. 7c), indicating that the RGB method for on-the-spot pH detection based on the pH-dependent film sensor is practicable. It also promotes the pH film sensor becoming prospect for the micro-volume pH detection.

Some of the reported alkaline pH sensors in its particular ranges and fluorescent detection methods are summarized in Table 1 , in which the alkaline pH detecting linear ranges of NU-1000-NH2 and its film are relatively wide, and covers the weak alkaline range among most reported methods. The application of proposed method to detect pH in real samples was investigated (the samples were obtained from local municipal wastewater treatment plant). As shown in Table 2 , the relative standard deviation (RSD) values of both proposed methods was from 0.17 % to 0.98 %, which were acceptable for the real practical samples pH detection.

In summary, for on-the-spot detection of water sample pH, the pyrene-based MOF of NU-1000-NH2 were successfully synthesized by using Zr metal, linker of ligand H4TBAPy and the introduction of amino alkaline group, in which the fluorescence emission originated from pyrene. And the fluorescence turn-on effect and emission blue shift in alkaline condition were observed in both NU-1000-NH2 and ligand H4TBAPy, which can be attributed to the deprotonation of the carboxylate groups of ligand. While, the excellent sensitivity and selectivity of trace concentration OH– ion of NU-1000-NH2 promoted it become the alkaline pH-dependent detection probe. Remarkably, the low-cost pH film sensor combining with the explored RGB equation of exacting RGB value by Photoshop, has brought a promising prospect for the on-the-spot pH detection of liquid drop. This work highlights the great potential and contribution of pyrene-based Zr-MOF to the build-up of pH sensing platform with its environmental monitoring in the future.

Hangzhou Li: Conceptualization, Methodology, Formal analysis, Investigation, Writing – original draft, Data curation, Visualization. Qiumeng Chen: Supervision, Validation, Writing – review & editing. Yuanyuan Wang: Investigation. Zhixiao Zhang: Investigation. Huan Chen: Supervision. Zhaoli Wang: Investigation. Zhengjun Gong: Conceptualization, Methodology, Supervision, Writing – review & editing, Project administration, Resources, Funding acquisition.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work is financially supported by the Science and Technology Department Foundation of Sichuan Province (No. 2022YFS0502, 2022NSFC0989 & 2022024) and the National Natural Science Foundation of China (No. 22176154). Thanks to the Analytical and Testing Center of Southwest Jiaotong University for the support of experimental conditions.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.microc.2023.108450.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
Supplementary data 1
